description: A large Arctic bear that depends on sea ice to hunt seals and travel between feeding grounds.
A small fox adapted to extreme cold, scavenging and hunting rodents and seabirds across tundra landscapes.
A tusked marine mammal that forages on the seafloor for clams and other invertebrates near sea-ice edges.
The largest penguin, breeding on sea ice and diving deep for fish and squid in frigid Southern Ocean waters.
A seal that maintains breathing holes in sea ice and feeds on fish and cephalopods under the ice.
A high-altitude cat with thick fur and long tail, hunting ibex and blue sheep across rugged alpine terrain.
A small arboreal mammal in mountain forests that feeds primarily on bamboo and seasonal fruits.
A keystone megaherbivore that shapes savanna ecosystems by browsing trees, grazing grasses, and digging for water.
A social big cat living in prides, preying on antelope and other ungulates in open grasslands and savannas.
The fastest land animal, specialized for high-speed chases of small to medium antelope in open habitats.
A tall browsing herbivore that feeds on acacia leaves and helps shape woodland structure through selective browsing.
A semi-aquatic grazer that spends days in water and forages on grasses at night, influencing nutrient flows.
A large crocodile and apex predator of African rivers and lakes, ambushing fish, birds, and mammals.
A great ape living in high-elevation forests, feeding on leaves, shoots, and stems in cohesive family groups.
A forest-dwelling relative of the giraffe, browsing understory plants in dense rainforests of the Congo Basin.
A highly intelligent primate using tools and complex social behavior, eating fruits, insects, and occasional meat.
A marine turtle grazing seagrass meadows and algae, connecting reef and coastal ecosystems through migration.
A reef-associated turtle with a narrow beak, feeding largely on sponges and helping maintain reef diversity.
A powerful big cat of the Americas, hunting across forests and wetlands and preying on capybaras and caimans.
A large forest raptor that hunts arboreal mammals like sloths and monkeys in tall tropical rainforest canopies.
A slow-moving arboreal mammal with low metabolism, feeding mainly on leaves and spending most time in trees.
A small amphibian with toxic skin alkaloids derived from diet, feeding on ants and other small invertebrates.
A large constrictor snake living in slow-moving waters, ambushing fish, birds, and mammals in wetlands.
The world's largest rodent, living in social groups near water and grazing aquatic plants and grasses.
A social mustelid of tropical rivers, hunting fish cooperatively and relying on clean waterways and riparian cover.
A large grazer that shapes prairie vegetation through grazing and wallowing, supporting grassland biodiversity.
A pack-hunting canid that preys on deer and elk, affecting trophic cascades in forest and mountain ecosystems.
A large raptor often near water, feeding on fish and waterfowl and using tall trees or cliffs for nesting.
A large deer adapted to cold regions, browsing shrubs and aquatic plants across boreal forests and marshes.
A widespread bear that eats berries, roots, insects, and fish, including salmon runs in northern rivers.
A nocturnal burrowing mammal that feeds on earthworms, insects, and fruits across mixed farmland and woods.
A large deer of forests and moorlands, grazing and browsing and serving as prey for large carnivores where present.
A highly specialized predator that feeds mainly on rabbits in Mediterranean shrublands and open woodlands.
A migratory deer adapted to cold climates, feeding on lichens and shrubs and traveling long distances seasonally.
An arboreal marsupial specializing on eucalyptus leaves, with low-energy lifestyle and strong habitat dependence.
A large hopping marsupial grazing grasses and forbs in open habitats, adapted to dry conditions and variable rainfall.
A semi-aquatic monotreme that forages underwater for insect larvae and small invertebrates in streams and rivers.
A small reef fish living among sea anemones, feeding on plankton and algae and benefiting from symbiosis.
A large monitor lizard that scavenges and hunts deer and pigs, using ambush tactics in warm, seasonal habitats.
A great ape living mostly in the forest canopy, feeding on fruit, leaves, and insects and sensitive to deforestation.
A critically endangered tiger adapted to dense forests, hunting deer and wild pigs in island rainforests.
A large one-horned rhinoceros inhabiting floodplain grasslands and wetlands, grazing and browsing in tall grasses.
A big cat found in diverse habitats including mangroves and forests, preying on deer and wild boar.
A bear with specialized diet of bamboo, living in mountainous forests and relying on intact bamboo understory.
A migratory crane breeding in Siberian wetlands, feeding on aquatic plants and invertebrates along flyways.
A baleen whale known for long migrations and complex songs, feeding on krill and small fish via bubble-net feeding.
The largest animal on Earth, feeding primarily on krill in productive cold-water upwelling zones.
A marine mammal grazing seagrass in shallow coastal waters, vulnerable to habitat loss and boat strikes.
A small desert fox with large ears for heat dissipation, feeding on insects, small vertebrates, and fruits.
A desert-adapted ungulate capable of long periods without water, browsing tough vegetation in arid landscapes.
A long-distance migratory seabird that breeds in Arctic regions and feeds on small fish and invertebrates.
A unique lizard that forages on marine algae and basks on volcanic rocks, adapted to saltwater environments.
A large predatory shark of temperate coastal waters, feeding on fish and marine mammals and patrolling seal colonies.
A large scavenging bird relying on carrion and open landscapes, historically threatened by lead poisoning.
A migratory butterfly whose larvae feed on milkweed, undertaking multi-generational migrations across North America.
A social lemur of Madagascar feeding on fruits, leaves, and insects, using scent communication and sunbathing.
A nocturnal lemur using an elongated finger to tap wood and extract insect larvae, adapted to forest foraging.
A large frugivorous bird that disperses seeds in rainforests, also eating small animals and nesting in tree cavities.
A freshwater dolphin navigating complex river channels and flooded forests, feeding on diverse fish species.
A migratory antelope of the Eurasian steppe with a distinctive nose that filters dust and warms cold air.
A venomous reef fish that ambushes small fish and crustaceans among coral crevices and rocky outcrops.
A small bony fish that clings to seagrass with a prehensile tail and feeds on tiny crustaceans by suction.
A large ray that glides through plankton-rich waters, filtering zooplankton and gathering at cleaning stations.
The largest fish, a gentle filter-feeder that follows plankton blooms and fish spawning events in warm seas.
An Arctic toothed whale known for its long tusk, feeding on fish and squid beneath sea ice.
A white whale that migrates along ice edges and estuaries, using rich vocalizations and echolocation to hunt.
A highly social apex predator with distinct ecotypes, hunting fish or marine mammals using coordinated tactics.
A deep-diving whale that hunts squid in the dark mesopelagic zone, using powerful echolocation clicks.
A large parrot of lowland forests that feeds on nuts and fruits and depends on mature trees for nesting cavities.
A colorful bird of montane forests that eats fruit—especially wild avocados—and nests in decaying tree trunks.
A canopy bird with a large bill, dispersing seeds by eating fruit and also taking insects and small prey.
A bright red wading bird feeding on crustaceans in coastal wetlands, nesting in large colonies in mangroves.
A massive soaring scavenger of the Andes that uses updrafts to travel long distances while searching for carrion.
A fast aerial predator that hunts birds mid-flight, now common in some cities where tall buildings mimic cliffs.
A powerful raptor of open country that hunts rabbits and ground squirrels and nests on cliffs or large trees.
A nocturnal owl with prominent ear tufts, taking diverse prey from rodents to birds across mixed landscapes.
A pale owl that hunts rodents by sound in open fields, often nesting in barns, cavities, or cliff crevices.
A large crane associated with marshes and river plains, feeding on aquatic invertebrates, fish, and plants.
A cold-adapted primate living in troops, foraging on fruits, seeds, insects, and seasonal plant foods.
A high-elevation monkey that relies on intact forests, feeding heavily on lichens, buds, and young leaves.
A grass-eating primate of Ethiopian plateaus, living in large social groups and grazing like ungulates.
A social mongoose of arid regions that cooperatively guards burrows and forages for insects and small vertebrates.
A resilient carnivore that hunts and scavenges, playing a major role in nutrient cycling across savanna ecosystems.
A cooperative pack hunter with high hunting success, vulnerable to habitat fragmentation and disease spillover.
A browsing rhinoceros that feeds on shrubs and small trees, threatened historically by poaching for its horn.
A large grazing rhinoceros of open grasslands, forming loose groups and relying on protected areas for survival.
A social grazer that migrates with seasonal rains, feeding on grasses and helping maintain open savanna structure.
A migratory antelope that moves in vast herds, tracking rainfall and driving grazing dynamics across plains.
A robust bovine living in herds, grazing grasses and using water sources that shape movement across landscapes.
A large antelope that browses and grazes, capable of long-distance movements in dry environments.
A desert-adapted marsupial that travels across open country, feeding on grasses and conserving water in hot climates.
A nocturnal scavenging marsupial that also hunts small prey, impacted by a contagious facial tumor disease.
A burrowing marsupial with a slow metabolism, feeding on grasses and roots and shaping soils through digging.
A flightless nocturnal bird that probes soil for invertebrates, vulnerable to introduced predators.
A critically endangered flightless parrot that breeds irregularly in mast years and relies on intensive conservation.
A long-lived tortoise that grazes grasses and shrubs, shaping island vegetation and dispersing seeds.
A coastal otter that hunts crabs and fish in intertidal zones, depending on clean rocky shores and kelp habitat.
A keystone predator that eats sea urchins and helps maintain kelp forests, using tools to crack hard-shelled prey.
A rare seal that forages on reef fish and cephalopods, resting on beaches and threatened by human disturbance.
A gentle aquatic mammal grazing seagrass and freshwater plants, vulnerable to boat strikes and habitat loss.
A seabird that nests in burrows on coastal islands and brings small fish to chicks, sensitive to changing fish stocks.
A long-winged seabird that travels vast distances on wind, feeding on squid and fish and threatened by bycatch.
A cold-adapted hare with seasonal coat color, feeding on willow and dry grasses on windswept tundra.
A shaggy tundra ungulate forming defensive circles against predators and grazing sedges and grasses.
A wide-ranging mustelid that scavenges and hunts in cold regions, requiring large intact landscapes.
A forest cat specialized on snowshoe hares, with large paws for traveling over deep snow.
A hare with seasonally white fur, feeding on twigs and bark in winter and forming key prey in boreal food webs.
An ecosystem engineer that builds dams, creating wetlands that increase habitat diversity for many species.
A social pinniped that hauls out on beaches and rocky islands, feeding on fish and squid in nearshore waters.
A widespread seal that forages on fish in shallow seas and rests on sandbanks, rocks, and tidal flats.
A semi-aquatic mustelid feeding mostly on fish, sensitive to water quality and riparian habitat continuity.
A dam-building rodent that creates ponds and wet meadows, improving water retention and habitat complexity.
A small spiny mammal that forages at night for insects and worms, benefitting from connected green spaces.
A burrowing herbivore that shapes plant communities and provides key prey for many predators in Mediterranean habitats.
A highly adaptable canid that hunts small mammals and eats fruit and carrion, thriving in rural and urban mosaics.
A fast-running hare of open farmland that relies on field margins and diverse crops for cover and forage.
A rare seal that rests in secluded sea caves and forages on fish and cephalopods, threatened by disturbance and habitat loss.
A large sea turtle with powerful jaws for crushing crustaceans, nesting on sandy beaches and migrating widely.
A tall wading bird that stalks fish and amphibians in shallow water, nesting in colonies near productive wetlands.
A large reptile of swamps and rivers, creating 'gator holes' that provide refuges for many species during drought.
A wading bird that filter-feeds on tiny crustaceans and algae in shallow salty lakes, forming large breeding colonies.
A nocturnal, scale-covered mammal that eats ants and termites, heavily threatened by illegal wildlife trade.
A specialized insectivore using a long tongue to harvest ants and termites, vulnerable to fires and road mortality.
A burrowing mammal with armored plates, foraging for insects and small invertebrates in leaf litter and soil.
A large nocturnal armadillo that excavates termite mounds and digs deep burrows used by other wildlife.
A large forest herbivore that disperses seeds over long distances, often using rivers as movement corridors.
A brightly colored parrot that feeds on fruits and seeds, nesting in large tree cavities and moving in noisy pairs.
A canopy primate known for loud calls, feeding on leaves and fruit and influencing forest regeneration through seed dispersal.
A spotted wild cat of dense cover that hunts small mammals and birds, sensitive to habitat fragmentation.
A wide-ranging predator that hunts deer and smaller prey, requiring large connected habitats for dispersal.
A large bear that forages on berries, roots, insects, and fish, influenced by salmon runs and seasonal foods.
A forest-dwelling bear with flexible diet, using mast crops like acorns and berries and sometimes interacting with human food.
A clever generalist that forages along waterways and in cities, eating fruit, insects, and human-associated foods.
A playful semi-aquatic predator feeding on fish and crayfish, indicating healthy aquatic ecosystems where present.
A rare one-horned rhinoceros surviving in a small protected area, browsing forest plants and relying on intact lowland habitat.
A forest megaherbivore that disperses seeds and opens pathways, affected by habitat loss and human-wildlife conflict.
A secretive forest cat with excellent climbing ability, hunting arboreal and terrestrial prey in dense forests.
A large nocturnal browser with distinctive black-and-white coloration, using forest rivers and trails for movement.
A smaller elephant population that relies on lowland forests and river valleys, threatened by conversion to plantations.
A river-edge primate adapted to mangroves, feeding on leaves and unripe fruits and swimming well between banks.
A large forest raptor that hunts arboreal mammals and birds, requiring extensive intact rainforest for nesting and foraging.
An arboreal great ape that builds nightly nests and feeds mostly on fruit, sensitive to logging and forest fragmentation.
A small ape that brachiates through the canopy and sings to defend territories, relying on continuous forest cover.
A large venomous snake that preys primarily on other snakes, occupying forest edges and disturbed habitats near cover.
A large crocodile that uses tidal channels and river mouths, ambushing fish, birds, and mammals in brackish waters.
A small lizard common in warm climates, feeding on insects and displaying color changes linked to temperature and stress.
A neotenic salamander retaining larval traits, feeding on aquatic invertebrates and threatened by water pollution and invasive fish.
The largest frog species, living near rapids and feeding on insects and small animals, vulnerable to habitat disturbance.
A large nocturnal scorpion that shelters in burrows and feeds on insects and small vertebrates in humid forests.
A large octopus that hunts crabs and mollusks on cold rocky bottoms, using camouflage and problem-solving behavior.
